description:
You are acting as an artifact-manager who curates in-house binary
  repositories.  
  A starter directory containing raw binaries has already been placed on
  the system for you:

      /home/user/artifacts-source/
          ├── alpha.bin   (ASCII text, 6 bytes, contains the string “ALPHA\n”)
          └── beta.bin    (ASCII text, 5 bytes, contains the string “BETA\n”)

  Your goal is to turn these two raw binaries into a **very small,
  file-system based repository** under

      /home/user/repositories/core/

  Carry out the following high-level steps (do **NOT** display the
  literal commands you run):

  1. Create the target directory tree if it does not yet exist.
  2. Copy both binaries from the source location into the new repository
     directory, keeping their original file-names.
  3. For every binary inside
         /home/user/repositories/core/
     calculate its SHA-256 checksum.
  4. Produce a plain-text index file named exactly
         /home/user/repositories/core/INDEX.txt
     that fulfils **all** of these formatting rules:
       • One binary per line.  
       • Lines must be sorted in *ascending alphabetical* order
         by the artifact’s file-name.  
       • The line format is three TAB-separated columns
         (that is, a single ASCII 0x09 character between the fields):

             <artifact_name><TAB><size_in_bytes>.<TAB><sha256sum>

         Example (spacing shown with “·” characters):
             alpha.bin·6·2b73564f…  
             beta.bin·5·35a39d3b…

       • **No** leading or trailing whitespace on any line.
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline (UNIX “\n”).
  5. Produce an operational log named
         /home/user/repositories/core/curation.log
     The log must contain *exactly two* lines — one for each binary —
     using the precise layout below:

         <UTC-ISO8601 timestamp>Z | <artifact_name> | <sha256sum>

     Formatting requirements:
       • The timestamp must be in UTC with the literal trailing “Z”
         (e.g. 2023-10-05T14:26:03Z).  
       • A single space, a vertical bar “|”, and another single space
         separate the three fields.  
       • Alphabetical order of the artifact-names (alpha.bin first).  
       • Only these two lines; end the file with one trailing “\n”.

  6. Verify your work by printing (to STDOUT) the contents of both
     INDEX.txt and curation.log so that a human reviewer can visually
     inspect them.  Do not add any extra commentary when you print.

  Notes & Constraints
  -------------------
  • You do *not* have root rights; stay entirely in /home/user.  
  • All paths in this task are absolute — use them verbatim.  
  • The automated checker will:
        - Ensure both files exist.
        - Parse and validate the exact formatting rules.
        - Independently recalculate the checksums for comparison.
  • If you need temporary workspace, create it inside /home/user/tmp
    and remove it afterwards.  
  • Avoid interactive programs; everything must run non-interactively.

  When you are done, double-check that:
    • /home/user/repositories/core/INDEX.txt exists and matches the spec
    • /home/user/repositories/core/curation.log exists and matches the spec
    • Both files list exactly “alpha.bin” and “beta.bin” in that order.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts
  -----------------------------------------
  Directory /home/user/artifacts-source/ already exists and contains:

    1. /home/user/artifacts-source/alpha.bin
         – plain ASCII text
         – exact byte sequence (including newline):
               41 4c 50 48 41 0a   # “ALPHA\n”
         – SHA-256 expected to be computed at runtime; the checker will
           calculate   sha256sum /home/user/artifacts-source/alpha.bin
           and store the value internally as SHA_ALPHA.

    2. /home/user/artifacts-source/beta.bin
         – plain ASCII text
         – exact byte sequence (including newline):
               42 45 54 41 0a       # “BETA\n”
         – SHA-256 will be computed at runtime and stored as SHA_BETA.

  Verification logic used by the automated tests
  ----------------------------------------------
  After the agent finishes, the checker will confirm:

    • Directory /home/user/repositories/core/ is present.

    • Both binaries have been copied there:
        /home/user/repositories/core/alpha.bin
        /home/user/repositories/core/beta.bin
      The checker will compare the copies byte-for-byte with the
      originals.

    • /home/user/repositories/core/INDEX.txt exists and satisfies:
        – exactly two lines, alphabetically sorted.
        – each line matches regex:
              ^(alpha\.bin|beta\.bin)\t[0-9]+\t[a-f0-9]{64}\n$
        – The size column must be “6” for alpha.bin and “5” for beta.bin.
        – The checksum column equals SHA_ALPHA for alpha.bin and
          SHA_BETA for beta.bin.

    • /home/user/repositories/core/curation.log exists and satisfies:
        – exactly two lines, alphabetically sorted.
        – each line matches the regex
              ^20[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z \| (alpha\.bin|beta\.bin) \| [a-f0-9]{64}\n$
        – the checksum at the end of each line must equal the
          corresponding SHA_ALPHA / SHA_BETA.

    • INDEX.txt and curation.log both end with a single trailing newline
      and contain no extra blank lines or whitespace.

  The tests intentionally recompute the SHA-256 checksums rather than
  storing static literal values so that they remain valid even if the
  underlying container image changes.
